appliedUpdates.description: Confirmed Flet CanvasKit limitation - tests use component-level mocking per existing pattern

VAL-CROSS-001.name: New User Registration Flow
VAL-CROSS-001.description: Complete new user registration flow from app start to home: setup password -> register -> login -> onboarding -> home

VAL-CROSS-002.name: Returning User Login Flow
VAL-CROSS-002.description: Returning user login flow: unlock -> login -> home with profile. Session expiry and profile corruption tested

VAL-CROSS-003.name: Complete Session Flow
VAL-CROSS-003.description: Complete meditation session: intro -> active -> concluded -> history. Session immutability and error handling tested

VAL-CROSS-004.name: View Session History Flow
VAL-CROSS-004.description: History viewing: history list -> session detail -> back. Empty state, pagination, and deleted session tested

VAL-CROSS-005.name: Edit Profile Flow
VAL-CROSS-005.description: Profile edit: load pre-filled -> edit -> save -> home. Validation failures and network error handling tested

VAL-CROSS-006.name: Change Settings Flow
VAL-CROSS-006.description: Settings changes: TTS/STT/AI model with auto-save. Rapid changes, invalid values, and persistence tested

VAL-CROSS-007.name: Logout Flow
VAL-CROSS-007.description: Logout from all pages: home, history, settings, profile. Immediate re-login and token invalidation tested

note: Tests use component-level testing with Python mocks (FastAPI TestClient + unittest.mock) rather than browser automation, following the established pattern in src/tests/test_cross_flows.py. This approach is required because Flet's CanvasKit renderer doesn't expose DOM elements for browser-based interaction testing.
